<!-- topic: hooks -->
<!-- sources: ADR-003, ADR-014, ADR-019, ADR-026, INV-003 -->
<!-- compiled_by: gov-compile v0.6.0-rc4 -->
<!-- compiled_at: 2026-05-04T09:46:01Z -->

# Hooks

[edikt:directives:start]: #
- A PostToolUse hook MUST fire after Write or Edit on source files. (ref: ADR-003)
- If a formatter is detected in the project (gofmt, prettier, black, rustfmt), it SHOULD run automatically. (ref: ADR-003)
- Linter config to AI rules translation MUST be implemented as a new command edikt:sync. (ref: ADR-003)
- MUST NOT fork commands that need to write files (edikt:init, edikt:context, edikt:adr, etc.) — forked subagents can't write back to the parent session. (ref: ADR-003)
- Shell preprocessing MUST be used in slash commands where live project state matters. (ref: ADR-003)
- The PostToolUse hook MUST only fire when the edited file matches a known source extension. (ref: ADR-003)
- The PostToolUse hook MUST suppress formatting when writing markdown, YAML, JSON config files. (ref: ADR-003)
- context: fork frontmatter MUST be added to commands that do read-only analysis and should not affect the main session. (ref: ADR-003)
- edikt:sync MUST run during edikt:init for brownfield projects (auto), and manually when linter config changes. (ref: ADR-003)
- ADR-011's test infrastructure decisions (opt-out polarity, _runner.sh immutability, _staged_runner.sh extension, fixture _note rationale) MUST carry forward unchanged. (ref: ADR-014)
- All plaintext-emitting edikt hooks MUST emit JSON conforming to the Claude Code hook protocol by the v0.5.0 release. (ref: ADR-014)
- Fixture expected outputs for migrated hooks MUST be regenerated by running the updated hook against its payload. (ref: ADR-014)
- Hand-authored expected outputs MUST NOT be used — they remain forbidden per ADR-011. (ref: ADR-014)
- Hook behavior refinements MUST each be covered by a characterization fixture pair before ship in v0.5.0. (ref: ADR-014)
- If a user-facing string needs rewording, it MAY be addressed as a separate commit with a separate fixture update, not folded into the wrapping migration. (ref: ADR-014)
- Migration MUST NOT change message content. (ref: ADR-014)
- The characterization test gate (EDIKT_SKIP_HOOK_TESTS=1 opt-out) from ADR-011 MUST remain opt-out. (ref: ADR-014)
- The user-visible message text MUST be preserved byte-for-byte inside the wrapping — migration is a transport change, not a content change. (ref: ADR-014)
- The v0.5.0 ship-gate MUST be the hook-level JSON migration plus fixtures.yaml §9.1 notes documenting the deferral rationale for the 2 pending pairs. (ref: ADR-014)
- When re-added, each fixture MUST document its verified_by command. (ref: ADR-014)
- Any future content change to a non-listed hook MUST follow ADR-014 unaltered (wrap first, content change separately). (ref: ADR-019)
- For these four hooks only, security-driven rewrites may change user-visible message content AND JSON wrapping format in the same commit, with fresh fixtures generated by running the updated hook against its payload. (ref: ADR-019)
- No other hook may invoke this carve-out. (ref: ADR-019)
- Style rewrites, prose polish, or UX improvements MUST NOT qualify for the security carve-out of ADR-019. (ref: ADR-019)
- The carve-out MUST be justified only when the existing content violates INV-003 or INV-004. (ref: ADR-019)
- All evaluator agents MUST emit structured JSON conforming to `templates/agents/evaluator-verdict.schema.json` by v0.5.0. (ref: ADR-026)
- Any project still running pre-v0.5.0 evaluator templates MUST update to the structured schema. (ref: ADR-026)
- If neither structured path yields a valid agent name, the hook MAY return `{"continue": true}` and exit — no severity detection, no threshold lookup, no gate firing. (ref: ADR-026)
- Specialist agents MUST be identified only via structured paths: `evaluator_output.agent` (ADR-023) or canonical Claude Code payload fields. (ref: ADR-026)
- Subagents that DO yield an identity MUST continue to fire gates per ADR-023 §4 threshold resolution. (ref: ADR-026)
- Subagents that yield no identity through either structured path MUST exit clean — no gate firing. (ref: ADR-026)
- The block in `templates/hooks/subagent-stop.sh` that scanned `INPUT_LOWER` for keywords like `architect`, `dba`, `security` MUST be removed in v0.6.0 (not v0.7.0). (ref: ADR-026)
- The block that grep'd for severity terms when `evaluator_output.severity` is absent MUST be removed from subagent-stop.sh. (ref: ADR-026)
- The fallback regex (`As (Staff|Senior|Principal) [A-Za-z]+`) in subagent-stop.sh MUST also be removed. (ref: ADR-026)
- The legacy value `grep-fallback` MUST be removed from the code; it MUST NOT appear in any new gate event. (ref: ADR-026)
[edikt:directives:end]: #
[edikt:directives:sha256]: # 9406db0bdfbb63e00d672b029a8f591c14265b4c811f08eafd1ddf3d3ebe8ab5

[edikt:prohibitions:start]: #
## Prohibitions
- MUST NOT fully supersede ADR-014 — removes the byte-for-byte protection still valid for the majority of hooks and opens the door to content drift during routine migration work. (ref: ADR-019)
- MUST NOT implement a heuristic slash-command subagent exemption (absent `subagent_type` + content detection) as a workaround — this replaces one content-detection heuristic with another. (ref: ADR-026)
- MUST NOT keep `gates.default` firing for non-agent subagents with no identity — a non-agent with no identity MUST NOT trigger any gate. (ref: ADR-026)
- MUST NOT land security rewrites as separate post-migration PRs — ships a known-Critical vulnerability between the wrapping PR and the security rewrite PR. (ref: ADR-019)
- MUST NOT leave edikt in a mixed state with some hooks emitting JSON and others emitting plaintext across a release cycle. (ref: ADR-014)
[edikt:prohibitions:end]: #
[edikt:prohibitions:sha256]: # f1253c25bd5006983abf6d10601fb8ecd24965e009a36130be14fd0c52fbb67f

[edikt:manual:start]: #
